title: Understand: electric and magnetic forces
description: Check your understanding of electric and magnetic forces in this set of free practice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
Charged objects $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ exert electric forces on each other.

**Complete the statements.**

* The more total charge $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have, the [[☃ dropdown 1]] the electric forces they exert on each other.

* The farther apart $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ are, the [[☃ dropdown 2]] the electric forces they exert on each other.
hints: The strength of the electric forces two objects exert on each other depends on the objects' total charges. The more charge one or both of the objects have, the stronger the electric forces they exert on each other.

The strength of the electric forces two objects exert on each other also depends on the distance between the objects. The farther apart the objects are, the weaker the electric forces they exert on each other.

The answer is:

* The more total charge $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have, the stronger the electric forces they exert on each other.

* The farther apart $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ are, the weaker the electric forces they exert on each other.

assessment_items.question_content:
Charged objects $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ exert electric forces on each other.

**Complete the statements.**

* The less total charge $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have, the [[☃ dropdown 1]] the electric forces they exert on each other.

* The closer together $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ are, the [[☃ dropdown 2]] the electric forces they exert on each other.
hints: The strength of the electric forces two objects exert on each other depends on the objects' total charges. The less charge one or both of the objects have, the weaker the electric forces they exert on each other.

The strength of the electric forces two objects exert on each other also depends on the distance between the objects. The closer together the objects are, the stronger the electric forces they exert on each other.

The answer is:

* The less total charge $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have, the weaker the electric forces they exert on each other.

* The closer together $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ are, the stronger the electric forces they exert on each other.

assessment_items.question_content:
Charged objects $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ exert electric forces on each other.

**Complete the statements.**

* The electric forces are stronger when $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have [[☃ dropdown 3]] and are [[☃ dropdown 4]].
hints: The strength of the electric forces two objects exert on each other depends on the objects' total charges. The more charge one or both of the objects have, the stronger the electric forces they exert on each other.

The strength of the electric forces two objects exert on each other also depends on the distance between the objects. The closer together the objects are, the stronger the electric forces they exert on each other.

The answer is:

* The electric forces are stronger when $\bold{A}$ and $\bold{B}$ have more total charge and are closer together.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the table for each pair of objects.**

**Description of objects** | **Will the objects attract or repel each other?**
:- | :-
two charged objects, both with positive charge | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
two charged objects, one with positive charge and the other with negative charge | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
two magnets, with the north pole of one facing the south pole of the other | [[☃ dropdown 4]]
two magnets, with their south poles facing each other | [[☃ dropdown 3]]
hints: Both electric and magnetic forces follow the rule that *likes repel and opposites attract*.
For electric forces, this rule means that two like (same) charges will repel each other, and two opposite charges will attract each other.
For magnetic forces, this rule means that two like (same) poles will repel each other, and two opposite poles will attract each other.

The answer is:

**Description of objects** | **Will the objects attract or repel each other?**
:- | :-
two charged objects, both with positive charge | repel
two charged objects, one with positive charge and the other with negative charge | attract
two magnets, with the north pole of one facing the south pole of the other | attract
two magnets, with their south poles facing each other| repel

hints: Both electric and magnetic forces follow the rule that *likes repel and opposites attract*.
For electric forces, this rule means that two like (same) charges will repel each other, and two opposite charges will attract each other.
For magnetic forces, this rule means that two like (same) poles will repel each other, and two opposite poles will attract each other.

The answer is:

**Description of objects** | **Will the objects attract or repel each other?**
:- | :-
two charged objects, one with positive charge and the other with negative charge | attract
two charged objects, both with negative charge | repel
two magnets, with the north pole of one facing the south pole of the other | attract
two magnets, with their north poles facing each other | repel

For electric forces, this rule means that two like (same) charges will repel each other, and two opposite charges will attract each other.
For magnetic forces, this rule means that two like (same) poles will repel each other, and two opposite poles will attract each other.

The answer is:

two magnets, with their south poles facing each other | repel

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about magnetic interactions.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All magnets have both a north and south pole.
Two objects can exert magnetic forces on each other without being in contact.
Permanent magnets are the only source of magnetic fields.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about magnetic interactions.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Some magnets have only a north pole, and no south pole.
Two objects must be in contact to exert magnetic forces on each other.
Magnetic forces can be either attractive or repulsive.
Both permanent magnets and electric currents produce magnetic fields.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about magnetic interactions.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Some magnets have only a south pole, and no north pole.
Two objects can exert magnetic forces on each other without being in contact.
Both permanent magnets and electric currents produce magnetic fields.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about electric interactions.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There are two types of electric charge.
Two objects can exert electric forces on each other without being in contact.
Only special forms of matter contain particles with electric charge.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about electric interactions.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is only one type of electric charge.
Electric forces can be either attractive or repulsive.
Two objects must be in contact to exert electric forces on each other.
Matter contains many small particles with electric charge.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Select the true statements about electric interactions.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is only one type of electric charge.
Two objects can exert electric forces on each other without being in contact.
